name: config_item_trigger_report
description:
Query the publish and trigger trend report for a config item. 
Use this tool when you need time-range report data for one config item, a template slice under that config item, or a strategy slice under that config item. This tool returns data report content, not config item metadata. 
Request tracking rule: `requestId` should be a UUID string so it is globally unique. If you do not provide it, the system generates one automatically. If you later need to cancel this query through the corresponding cancellation capability, you must reuse the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query. 
Slice rules: leave both `templateIdList` and `strategyIdList` empty to query at config-item level; pass `templateIdList` to narrow the report to one or more templates; pass `strategyIdList` to narrow the report to one or more strategies. 
Return shape: `ConfigItemTriggerDataResponseDTO`, with top-level `resultGenerateTime` and nested `data` containing the trigger/report payload.

arguments.description: Project ID that owns the config item and scopes the query.
arguments.name: projectId

arguments.description: Config item ID to query.
arguments.name: configId

arguments.description: Optional request identifier for this query. Use a UUID string and keep it globally unique across queries. If omitted, the system generates a UUID automatically. If you later need to cancel this query, you must use the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query.
arguments.name: requestId

arguments.description: Template ID list used to narrow the report to specific templates under the config item. Leave it empty when you want config-level data or strategy-level data. Do not pass this together with a non-empty `strategyIdList`. Blank items are ignored.
arguments.name: templateIdList

arguments.description: Strategy ID list used to narrow the report to specific strategies under the config item. Leave it empty when you want config-level data or template-level data. Do not pass this together with a non-empty `templateIdList`. Blank items are ignored.
arguments.name: strategyIdList

arguments.description: Start date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format.
arguments.name: startTime

arguments.description: End date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format. It should be the same as or later than `startTime`.
arguments.name: endTime

arguments.description: Display timezone offset in hours, for example `8.0` or `-5.0`. Pass the business reporting timezone when the query should not use the current user context timezone.
arguments.name: showTimeZone

arguments.description: Internal trigger-report calculation switch. Supported values: `true` or `false`. Use `true` only when you explicitly need the internal analysis-report query path. Default: if omitted, the MCP layer uses `false`.
arguments.name: analyzeReportInternalQuery

name: config_item_analysis_report
description:
Query the analysis report for a config item. 
Use this tool when you need analysis report metrics for one config item, a template slice, or a strategy slice. This tool returns report data, not config item metadata. 
Request tracking rule: `requestId` should be a UUID string so it is globally unique. If you do not provide it, the system generates one automatically. If you later need to cancel this query through the corresponding cancellation capability, you must reuse the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query. 
Slice rules: leave both `templateIdList` and `strategyIdList` empty to query at config-item level; pass `templateIdList` to narrow the report to one or more templates; pass `strategyIdList` to narrow the report to one or more strategies. 
Return shape: `ConfigItemAnalyzeResDTO`, with top-level `resultGenerateTime` and nested `data` containing the analysis report payload.

arguments.description: Project ID that owns the config item and scopes the query.
arguments.name: projectId

arguments.description: Config item ID to query.
arguments.name: configId

arguments.description: Optional request identifier for this query. Use a UUID string and keep it globally unique across queries. If omitted, the system generates a UUID automatically. If you later need to cancel this query, you must use the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query.
arguments.name: requestId

arguments.description: Template ID list used to narrow the report to specific templates under the config item. Leave it empty when you want config-level data or strategy-level data. Do not pass this together with a non-empty `strategyIdList`. Blank items are ignored.
arguments.name: templateIdList

arguments.description: Strategy ID list used to narrow the report to specific strategies under the config item. Leave it empty when you want config-level data or template-level data. Do not pass this together with a non-empty `templateIdList`. Blank items are ignored.
arguments.name: strategyIdList

arguments.description: Start date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format.
arguments.name: startTime

arguments.description: End date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format. It should be the same as or later than `startTime`.
arguments.name: endTime

arguments.description: Display timezone offset in hours, for example `8.0` or `-5.0`. Pass the business reporting timezone when the query should not use the current user context timezone.
arguments.name: showTimeZone

name: config_item_strategy_comparison
description:
Query the strategy comparison report under one config item. 
Use this tool when the question is about relative performance across multiple strategies under the same config item. This tool is for side-by-side strategy comparison, not for config item metadata lookup. 
Request tracking rule: `requestId` should be a UUID string so it is globally unique. If you do not provide it, the system generates one automatically. If you later need to cancel this query through the corresponding cancellation capability, you must reuse the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query. 
Return shape: `ConfigItemCompareResDTO`, with top-level `resultGenerateTime` and nested `data` containing the strategy comparison payload.

arguments.description: Project ID that owns the config item and scopes the query.
arguments.name: projectId

arguments.description: Config item ID to query.
arguments.name: configId

arguments.description: Optional request identifier for this query. Use a UUID string and keep it globally unique across queries. If omitted, the system generates a UUID automatically. If you later need to cancel this query, you must use the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query.
arguments.name: requestId

arguments.description: Strategy ID list to compare under the same config item. Pass at least two non-blank strategy IDs when you want a meaningful side-by-side comparison. Blank items are ignored.
arguments.name: strategyIdList

arguments.description: Display timezone offset in hours, for example `8.0` or `-5.0`. Pass the business reporting timezone when the query should not use the current user context timezone.
arguments.name: showTimeZone

name: flow_node_overview_report
description:
Query the node-level overview report for all nodes in a flow canvas. 
Request tracking rule: `requestId` should be a UUID string so it is globally unique. If you do not provide it, the system generates one automatically. If you later need to cancel this query through the corresponding cancellation capability, you must reuse the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query. 
Use this tool for cross-node comparison inside one flow version. It returns all-node overview data rather than detail for one node.

arguments.description: Project ID that owns the flow and scopes the query.
arguments.name: projectId

arguments.description: Flow ID. You may omit it if `flowUuid` is provided.
arguments.name: flowId

arguments.description: Flow UUID of the target flow version. You may omit it if `flowId` is provided.
arguments.name: flowUuid

arguments.description: Optional request identifier for this query. Use a UUID string and keep it globally unique across queries. If omitted, the system generates a UUID automatically. If you later need to cancel this query, you must use the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query.
arguments.name: requestId

arguments.description: Optional push content language code used to scope the report to a specific language version of the push copy. This is useful when the flow uses multilingual push content. Use Hermes push-language codes such as `default`, `en`, `zh-Hans`, `zh-Hant`, `ja`, or `ko`. If omitted, the MCP layer passes `null`, which means querying data across all languages.
arguments.name: pushLanguageCode

arguments.description: Data dimension type used by the report. Supported values are exactly: `uv` for unique-user counting and `pv` for occurrence/event counting. Default: if omitted, the MCP layer uses `uv`.
arguments.name: dataDimType

arguments.description: Start date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format.
arguments.name: startTime

arguments.description: End date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format. It should be the same as or later than `startTime`.
arguments.name: endTime

arguments.description: Display timezone offset for report rendering. Recommended format is `8.0` or `-5.0`. If omitted, the backend uses the current user context timezone.
arguments.name: showTimeZone

name: flow_process_report
description:
Query the process-level report for a flow canvas. 
Use this tool for flow-wide process analytics rather than node drill-down. 
Request tracking rule: `requestId` should be a UUID string so it is globally unique. If you do not provide it, the system generates one automatically. If you later need to cancel this query through the corresponding cancellation capability, you must reuse the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query. 
Supported `reportType` values are exactly: `overview`, `detail`, `exit_detail`, and `push_detail`. 
`overview` returns the high-level process summary. `detail` returns the standard process detail report. `exit_detail` returns exit-reason detail. `push_detail` returns push-detail data. 
Return shape: `FlowProcessReportMcpResponseDTO`. `overview` uses `overviewReport`; the other report types use `detailReport`.

arguments.description: Project ID that owns the flow and scopes the query.
arguments.name: projectId

arguments.description: Flow ID. Use this when querying the current logical flow identity. You may omit it if `flowUuid` is provided.
arguments.name: flowId

arguments.description: Flow UUID of a specific flow canvas version. Use this when version-level precision matters. You may omit it if `flowId` is sufficient.
arguments.name: flowUuid

arguments.description: Optional request identifier for this query. Use a UUID string and keep it globally unique across queries. If omitted, the system generates a UUID automatically. If you later need to cancel this query, you must use the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query.
arguments.name: requestId

arguments.description: Optional push content language code used to scope the report to a specific language version of the push copy. This is useful when the flow uses multilingual push content. Use Hermes push-language codes such as `default`, `en`, `zh-Hans`, `zh-Hant`, `ja`, or `ko`. If omitted, the MCP layer passes `null`, which means querying data across all languages.
arguments.name: pushLanguageCode

arguments.description: Process report type selector. Supported values are exactly: `overview`, `detail`, `exit_detail`, `push_detail`.
arguments.name: reportType

arguments.description: Data dimension type used by the report. Supported values are exactly: `uv` for unique-user counting and `pv` for occurrence/event counting. Default: if omitted, the MCP layer uses `uv`.
arguments.name: dataDimType

arguments.description: Start date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format. Required when `reportType` is `detail`, `exit_detail`, or `push_detail`. For `overview`, you may omit both `startTime` and `endTime`, or provide both together.
arguments.name: startTime

arguments.description: End date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format. It should be the same as or later than `startTime`. Required when `reportType` is `detail`, `exit_detail`, or `push_detail`. For `overview`, you may omit both `startTime` and `endTime`, or provide both together.
arguments.name: endTime

arguments.description: Display timezone offset for report rendering. Recommended format is `8.0` or `-5.0`. If omitted, the backend uses the current user context timezone.
arguments.name: showTimeZone

name: flow_node_detail_report
description:
Query the detailed report for one node in a flow canvas. 
Request tracking rule: `requestId` should be a UUID string so it is globally unique. If you do not provide it, the system generates one automatically. If you later need to cancel this query through the corresponding cancellation capability, you must reuse the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query. 
Use this tool for node drill-down after identifying a target node. It returns one-node detail data rather than all-node overview.

arguments.description: Project ID that owns the flow and scopes the query.
arguments.name: projectId

arguments.description: Flow ID. You may omit it if `flowUuid` is provided.
arguments.name: flowId

arguments.description: Flow UUID of the target flow version. You may omit it if `flowId` is provided.
arguments.name: flowUuid

arguments.description: Optional request identifier for this query. Use a UUID string and keep it globally unique across queries. If omitted, the system generates a UUID automatically. If you later need to cancel this query, you must use the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query.
arguments.name: requestId

arguments.description: Optional push content language code used to scope the report to a specific language version of the push copy. This is useful when the flow uses multilingual push content. Use Hermes push-language codes such as `default`, `en`, `zh-Hans`, `zh-Hant`, `ja`, or `ko`. If omitted, the MCP layer passes `null`, which means querying data across all languages.
arguments.name: pushLanguageCode

arguments.description: Node UUID to drill into. This parameter is required for a true node-level detail query.
arguments.name: nodeUuid

arguments.description: Branch ID for branch-aware nodes. Provide it when the node has multiple branches and you want one branch specifically. Leave it empty for non-branch nodes.
arguments.name: branchId

arguments.description: Indicator name used by some node detail views, for example user-detail or segment-oriented indicator labels. Leave it empty when the target node detail route does not require a specific indicator.
arguments.name: indicatorName

arguments.description: Data dimension type used by the report. Supported values are exactly: `uv` for unique-user counting and `pv` for occurrence/event counting. Default: if omitted, the MCP layer uses `uv`.
arguments.name: dataDimType

arguments.description: Start date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format.
arguments.name: startTime

arguments.description: End date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format. It should be the same as or later than `startTime`.
arguments.name: endTime

arguments.description: Display timezone offset for report rendering. Recommended format is `8.0` or `-5.0`. If omitted, the backend uses the current user context timezone.
arguments.name: showTimeZone

name: flow_ab_split_node_report
description:
Query the report for one AB split node in a flow canvas. 
This tool is for AB split node analytics inside a flow, not for standalone experiment management. Use it when you need branch-level performance data for an AB split node. 
Request tracking rule: `requestId` should be a UUID string so it is globally unique. If you do not provide it, the system generates one automatically. If you later need to cancel this query through the corresponding cancellation capability, you must reuse the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query. 
Supported `reportType` values are exactly: `overview` and `detail`. `overview` returns the summary comparison across AB branches. `detail` returns the detailed branch report table for the AB split node. 
Return shape: `FlowAbTestReportMcpResponseDTO`. `overview` uses `overviewReport`; `detail` uses `detailReport`.

arguments.description: Project ID that owns the flow and scopes the query.
arguments.name: projectId

arguments.description: Flow ID. You may omit it if `flowUuid` is provided.
arguments.name: flowId

arguments.description: Flow UUID of the target flow version. You may omit it if `flowId` is provided.
arguments.name: flowUuid

arguments.description: Optional request identifier for this query. Use a UUID string and keep it globally unique across queries. If omitted, the system generates a UUID automatically. If you later need to cancel this query, you must use the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query.
arguments.name: requestId

arguments.description: Optional push content language code used to scope the report to a specific language version of the push copy. This is useful when the flow uses multilingual push content. Use Hermes push-language codes such as `default`, `en`, `zh-Hans`, `zh-Hant`, `ja`, or `ko`. If omitted, the MCP layer passes `null`, which means querying data across all languages.
arguments.name: pushLanguageCode

arguments.description: AB split node report type selector. Supported values are exactly: `overview` and `detail`.
arguments.name: reportType

arguments.description: AB split node UUID to drill into.
arguments.name: nodeUuid

arguments.description: Indicator UUID inside the AB split node. Provide it when the node exposes multiple indicators and you want one indicator specifically. Leave it empty otherwise.
arguments.name: indicatorsUuid

arguments.description: Start date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format.
arguments.name: startTime

arguments.description: End date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format. It should be the same as or later than `startTime`.
arguments.name: endTime

arguments.description: Display timezone offset for report rendering. Recommended format is `8.0` or `-5.0`. If omitted, the backend uses the current user context timezone.
arguments.name: showTimeZone

name: flow_node_config_schema
description:
Query node config schema for save_flow/save_submit_flow. 
Returns type-specific schema, required fields, conditional rules, enum definitions, and three kinds of examples: minimal_valid, full_valid, common_invalid. 
Use this tool before constructing node.config to reduce invalid requests. 
Input: nodeType (required). Returns the schema for that node type only to avoid returning an oversized payload.

arguments.description: Optional node type filter, e.g., single_trigger, message_push, event_split_flow
arguments.name: nodeType

name: flow_node_config
description:
Validate one node.config before save_flow/save_submit_flow. 
This tool performs local static validation against query_flow_node_config_schema and selected runtime-aligned rules. 
It returns whether the config is valid, normalizedConfigObject, error list, and warnings for checks that still depend on live channel/template data. 
Use operationMode=save_flow or save_submit_flow so mode-specific required fields can be validated.

arguments.description: Node type, e.g., single_trigger, message_push, event_split_flow
arguments.name: nodeType

arguments.description: Node config JSON string. The top-level value must be a JSON object string.
arguments.name: config

arguments.description: Validation mode: save_flow or save_submit_flow
arguments.name: operationMode

name: channel
description:
Create a new channel. 
Input parameters: 
req (ChannelNewDTO): Channel creation request object. Fields in req: 
projectId (Integer): Project ID in request body. The final effective value is the top-level projectId parameter. 
channelType (Integer): Channel type. 
channelSubBizType (String): Channel business sub-type, such as webhook, fcm, aurora, apns, client, wechat_mini_game, dou_yin_recommended_game_card. 
channelName (String): Channel display name. 
pushIdType (String): Field name used as push target ID. 
config (String): Channel configuration JSON string. 
enableTouchEvent (Integer): Touch funnel switch. 
touchEventSource (String): Touch event source. 
eventDeliveryName (String): Actual delivery event name. 
eventClickName (String): Click event name. 
Returns a ChannelNewResponseDTO object containing: 
channelId (String): New channel ID.

arguments.description: Project ID
arguments.name: projectId

arguments.description: Channel creation request object
arguments.name: req

name: task_data_overview
description:
Query the high-level data overview for one operation task. 
This tool returns funnel-style summary data, not task configuration metadata. Use it when you need the headline performance stages for one task before drilling into detailed reports. 
Request tracking rule: `requestId` should be a UUID string so it is globally unique. If you do not provide it, the system generates one automatically. If you later need to cancel this query through the corresponding cancellation capability, you must reuse the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query. 
Routing rules: `taskType=normal` uses the standard task overview route for scheduled or manual tasks; `taskType=trigger` uses the triggered-task funnel route for event-driven tasks. 
Return shape: `TaskOverviewMcpResponseDTO` with `dataList` and `resultGenerateTime`. Each item in `dataList` has `key` and `value`. 
The stage keys in `dataList` are produced by the backend route and differ by task type. In practice, normal-task overview keys and trigger-task funnel keys are not guaranteed to be identical, so callers should parse by returned `key` values instead of assuming one fixed stage set.

arguments.description: Project ID that owns the task and scopes the query.
arguments.name: projectId

arguments.description: Task ID to query. Use a standard operation task ID when `taskType=normal`, and a triggered task ID when `taskType=trigger`.
arguments.name: taskId

arguments.description: Optional request identifier for this query. Use a UUID string and keep it globally unique across queries. If omitted, the system generates a UUID automatically. If you later need to cancel this query, you must use the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query.
arguments.name: requestId

arguments.description: Optional push content language code used to scope the report to a specific language version of the push copy. This is useful when the task has multilingual content. Use Hermes push-language codes such as `default`, `en`, `zh-Hans`, `zh-Hant`, `ja`, or `ko`. If omitted, the MCP layer passes `null`, which means querying data across all languages.
arguments.name: pushLanguageCode

arguments.description: Task type selector. Supported values are exactly: `normal` for scheduled or manual operation tasks, `trigger` for event-triggered operation tasks. This parameter decides which backend overview logic is used.
arguments.name: taskType

arguments.description: Data dimension type. Supported values are exactly: `uv` for unique-user counting and `pv` for occurrence/event counting. Default: if omitted, the MCP layer uses `uv`. This parameter is passed to the normal-task overview route. The trigger-task overview route does not use this field, so it can be omitted when `taskType=trigger`.
arguments.name: dataDimType

arguments.description: Display timezone offset for report rendering. Recommended format is `8.0` or `-5.0`. If omitted, the backend uses the current user context timezone.
arguments.name: showTimeZone

name: task_data_detail
description:
Query the detailed data report for one operation task. 
This tool returns report data, not task configuration metadata. Use it when you need date-level, execution-instance-level, or single-instance-daily detail after reviewing the task overview. 
Request tracking rule: `requestId` should be a UUID string so it is globally unique. If you do not provide it, the system generates one automatically. If you later need to cancel this query through the corresponding cancellation capability, you must reuse the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query. 
Routing rules: 
- `taskType=normal`: supported `detailType` values are `time`, `instance`, and `instance_daily`; 
- `taskType=trigger`: only `detailType=time` is supported, and you can further control the trigger report layout with `dataViewType`. 
Return shape: `TaskDataResponseDTO`, with top-level `resultGenerateTime` and nested `data`. The nested `data` object contains report axes and values, including fields such as `x`, `xTag`, `y`, and `z`.

arguments.description: Project ID that owns the task and scopes the query.
arguments.name: projectId

arguments.description: Task ID to query. Use a standard operation task ID when `taskType=normal`, and a triggered task ID when `taskType=trigger`.
arguments.name: taskId

arguments.description: Optional request identifier for this query. Use a UUID string and keep it globally unique across queries. If omitted, the system generates a UUID automatically. If you later need to cancel this query, you must use the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query.
arguments.name: requestId

arguments.description: Optional push content language code used to scope the report to a specific language version of the push copy. This is useful when the task has multilingual content. Use Hermes push-language codes such as `default`, `en`, `zh-Hans`, `zh-Hant`, `ja`, or `ko`. If omitted, the MCP layer passes `null`, which means querying data across all languages.
arguments.name: pushLanguageCode

arguments.description: Task type selector. Supported values are exactly: `normal` for scheduled or manual operation tasks, `trigger` for event-triggered operation tasks.
arguments.name: taskType

arguments.description: Detail mode selector. Supported values are exactly: `time`, `instance`, `instance_daily`. Rules: when `taskType=normal`, all three values are supported; when `taskType=trigger`, only `time` is supported.
arguments.name: detailType

arguments.description: Task instance ID for a single execution batch. This parameter is required only when `detailType=instance_daily`. For `time`, `instance`, or any trigger-task query, leave it empty.
arguments.name: taskInstanceId

arguments.description: Start date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format. The report includes data from this date.
arguments.name: startTime

arguments.description: End date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format. It should be the same as or later than `startTime`.
arguments.name: endTime

arguments.description: Data dimension type used by the report. Supported values are exactly: `uv` for unique-user counting and `pv` for occurrence/event counting. Default: if omitted, the MCP layer uses `uv`.
arguments.name: dataDimType

arguments.description: Retention mode selector. Supported values are exactly: `retention` for conversion-style retention and `lost` for churn/loss-style retention. Leave it empty when retention analysis is not needed.
arguments.name: retentionType

arguments.description: Triggered-task detail view type. This parameter is used only when `taskType=trigger` and `detailType=time`. Supported values are exactly: `2` for date view and `3` for trigger view. Default: if omitted in a trigger-task detail query, the MCP layer uses `2`. Leave it empty for normal-task detail queries.
arguments.name: dataViewType

arguments.description: Display timezone offset for report rendering. Recommended format is `8.0` or `-5.0`. If omitted, the backend uses the current user context timezone.
arguments.name: showTimeZone

name: task_metric_detail
description:
Query the metric-oriented detail report for one operation task. 
Use this tool when you want a table organized around metric definitions and grouping dimensions rather than the default task detail report layout. 
Request tracking rule: `requestId` should be a UUID string so it is globally unique. If you do not provide it, the system generates one automatically. If you later need to cancel this query through the corresponding cancellation capability, you must reuse the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query. 
Routing rules: `taskType=normal` uses the standard task metric route; `taskType=trigger` uses the triggered-task metric route. 
Return shape: `MetricDataDetailResponseDTO`, with top-level `resultGenerateTime` and nested `data`. The nested `data` object contains metric report fields including `x`, `headers`, `total`, `values`, `metricMap`, `expGroupList`, `z`, and `horseRaceInstanceIdList`.

arguments.description: Project ID that owns the task and scopes the query.
arguments.name: projectId

arguments.description: Task ID to query. Use a standard operation task ID when `taskType=normal`, and a triggered task ID when `taskType=trigger`.
arguments.name: taskId

arguments.description: Optional request identifier for this query. Use a UUID string and keep it globally unique across queries. If omitted, the system generates a UUID automatically. If you later need to cancel this query, you must use the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query.
arguments.name: requestId

arguments.description: Optional push content language code used to scope the report to a specific language version of the push copy. This is useful when the task has multilingual content. Use Hermes push-language codes such as `default`, `en`, `zh-Hans`, `zh-Hant`, `ja`, or `ko`. If omitted, the MCP layer passes `null`, which means querying data across all languages.
arguments.name: pushLanguageCode

arguments.description: Task type selector. Supported values are exactly: `normal` for scheduled or manual operation tasks, `trigger` for event-triggered operation tasks.
arguments.name: taskType

arguments.description: Start date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format.
arguments.name: startTime

arguments.description: End date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format. It should be the same as or later than `startTime`.
arguments.name: endTime

arguments.description: List of metric setting IDs to query. Pass specific metric setting IDs when you want a controlled metric subset. Blank items are ignored. Pass `null` or an empty list to let the backend choose its default metric set.
arguments.name: metricIdList

arguments.description: Metric grouping mode. Supported values are exactly: `1` for batch grouping, `2` for date grouping, `3` for trigger grouping, `4` for experiment-effect grouping. Default: if omitted, the MCP layer uses `1`.
arguments.name: groupType

arguments.description: Display timezone offset for report rendering. Recommended format is `8.0` or `-5.0`. If omitted, the backend uses the current user context timezone.
arguments.name: showTimeZone

name: task_experiment_report
description:
Query the experiment report for one operation task. 
Use this tool only when the task has experiment groups and you need experiment-specific performance data. This tool does not return task configuration metadata. 
Request tracking rule: `requestId` should be a UUID string so it is globally unique. If you do not provide it, the system generates one automatically. If you later need to cancel this query through the corresponding cancellation capability, you must reuse the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query. 
Routing rules: `taskType=normal` uses the standard task experiment route; `taskType=trigger` uses the triggered-task experiment route. 
Report modes: `reportType=overview` returns the summary comparison across experiment groups; `reportType=detail` returns the detailed experiment table. 
Return shape: `TaskExpReportMcpResponseDTO`. Overview mode mainly uses `overviewData`, `successUser`, and `resultGenerateTime`. Detail mode mainly uses `detailData` and `resultGenerateTime`. The response also echoes `taskType` and `reportType` for easier downstream routing.

arguments.description: Project ID that owns the task and scopes the query.
arguments.name: projectId

arguments.description: Task ID to query. Use a standard operation task ID when `taskType=normal`, and a triggered task ID when `taskType=trigger`.
arguments.name: taskId

arguments.description: Optional request identifier for this query. Use a UUID string and keep it globally unique across queries. If omitted, the system generates a UUID automatically. If you later need to cancel this query, you must use the same `requestId` that was actually used for this query.
arguments.name: requestId

arguments.description: Optional push content language code used to scope the report to a specific language version of the push copy. This is useful when the task has multilingual content. Use Hermes push-language codes such as `default`, `en`, `zh-Hans`, `zh-Hant`, `ja`, or `ko`. If omitted, the MCP layer passes `null`, which means querying data across all languages.
arguments.name: pushLanguageCode

arguments.description: Task type selector. Supported values are exactly: `normal` for scheduled or manual operation tasks, `trigger` for event-triggered operation tasks.
arguments.name: taskType

arguments.description: Experiment report type selector. Supported values are exactly: `overview` for high-level experiment group comparison and `detail` for the detailed experiment table.
arguments.name: reportType

arguments.description: Start date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format.
arguments.name: startTime

arguments.description: End date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format. It should be the same as or later than `startTime`.
arguments.name: endTime
